title: htmxr
text: Consulter dev/hyperverse.md en début de session — état et roadmap de tous les packages hyperverse. Mettre à jour ce fichier au fil des échanges.

title: htmxr
text: htmxr est un package R qui fournit des primitives pour construire des applications web avec htmx et plumber2. Il est agnostique CSS — il ne dépend d’aucun framework CSS particulier.

title: htmxr
text: R avec conventions tidyverse plumber2 comme serveur HTTP (pas plumber v1) htmx pour les interactions client htmltools pour la génération HTML roxygen2 pour la documentation

title: htmxr
code: htmxr/ ├── R/ │   ├── hx_attrs.R          # hx_attrs() — helper interne hx-* attributes │   ├── hx_example.R        # hx_run_example() — lance un exemple │   ├── hx_page.R           # hx_page(), hx_head() — page HTML complète │   ├── hx_serve_assets.R   # hx_serve_assets() — assets statiques │   ├── hx_slider_input.R   # hx_slider_input() — <input type="range"> │   └── reexports.R         # réexports htmltools ├── inst/ │   ├── assets/ │   │   └── htmx/ │   │       └── 2.0.8/ │   │           └── htmx.min.js │   └── examples/ │       └── hello/ │           └── api.R ├── man/ ├── DESCRIPTION └── NAMESPACE

title: htmxr
text: Les exemples disponibles se trouvent dans inst/examples/. Chaque sous-dossier contient un fichier api.R exécutable via hx_run_example("<nom>"). Les exemples existants sont une bonne source d’inspiration pour comprendre comment combiner les primitives htmxr. Consulter ce dossier avant de demander des exemples ou de l’aide.

title: htmxr
text: Les dossiers d’exemples sont nommés selon le concept pédagogique démontré, pas selon le dataset ou le domaine métier utilisé. ✅ select-input — démontre hx_select_input() + filtrage dynamique ✅ lazy-load — démontre le pattern tbody vide chargé au trigger="load" ❌ diamonds-explorer — nom de usecase, pas de concept

title: htmxr
text: Fonctions préfixées hx_ — pas htmxr_. Cette convention s’applique à toutes les fonctions exportées sans exception, y compris les helpers et les wrappers (ex : hx_is_htmx(), pas htmxr_is_htmx()). Ordre des paramètres des composants : id toujours en premier et obligatoire, label = NULL en deuxième. Cohérent avec Shiny (inputId en premier) et entre toutes les fonctions hx_* (ex : hx_button(id, label = NULL), hx_select_input(id, label = NULL, choices)). Paramètres htmx : get, post, target, swap, trigger, indicator, swap_oob, confirm Pas de paste0() pour construire du HTML — utiliser htmltools::tags Dans les routes plumber2, le paramètre réponse doit s’appeler response et le paramètre requête request — ce sont les seuls noms reconnus pour l’injection automatique (documenté dans la vignette plumber2 “routing--input”)

title: htmxr
text: Toujours lire la documentation avant de proposer une solution Tester les fonctions dans la console R avant de les proposer Ne pas affirmer quelque chose sans l’avoir vérifié Le package est CSS agnostique — ne pas imposer Bootstrap ou Tailwind

what: Build Modern Web Applications with htmx and plumber2
title: Build Modern Web Applications with htmx and plumber2
text: Build modern web applications R — without writing JavaScript. htmx handles client-side interactions via HTML attributes. plumber2 handles R endpoints server-side. htmxr bridges two R helpers generate right HTML wire everything together.

title: Build Modern Web Applications with htmx and plumber2
code: install.packages("htmxr")  # or install the development version from GitHub: pak::pak("hyperverse-r/htmxr")

title: Build Modern Web Applications with htmx and plumber2
text: first load, server returns complete HTML page. user interacts element (e.g. slider), htmx sends request R endpoint swaps response DOM — JavaScript needed, full page reload.

title: Build Modern Web Applications with htmx and plumber2
text: hello example ships htmxr. reproduces classic Old Faithful histogram slider control number bins.

title: Build Modern Web Applications with htmx and plumber2
text: GET / endpoint returns full HTML page built hx_page() hx_head(). slider created hx_slider_input(), generates <input type="range"> wired htmx attributes.
code: #* @get / #* @parser none #* @serializer html function() {   hx_page(     hx_head(title = "Old Faithful Geyser Data", bootstrap_css),     tags$div(       class = "container py-5",       # ...       hx_slider_input(         id = "bins",         label = "Number of bins:",         value = 30,         min = 1,         max = 50,         get = "/plot",         trigger = "input changed delay:300ms",         target = "#plot"       )       # ...     )   ) }

title: Build Modern Web Applications with htmx and plumber2
text: GET /plot endpoint standard plumber2 handler. receives bins query parameter, generates SVG histogram, returns HTML fragment.
code: generate_plot <- function(bins = 30) {   svg_string <- svglite::xmlSVG({     x <- faithful[, 2]     bins_seq <- seq(min(x), max(x), length.out = as.numeric(bins) + 1)     hist(x, breaks = bins_seq, col = "darkgray", border = "white")   })   svg_string }  #* @get /plot #* @query bins:integer(30) #* @parser none #* @serializer none function(query) {   generate_plot(query$bins) }

title: Build Modern Web Applications with htmx and plumber2
code: hx_run_example("hello")

title: Build Modern Web Applications with htmx and plumber2
text: htmxr built around explicit constraints: Primitives, framework — htmxr provides low-level building blocks. doesn’t impose page structure, routing convention, application model. compose app small, predictable pieces. HTML abstraction — Every hx_* function produces standard HTML elements hx-* attributes. hidden layer, reactivity graph, virtual DOM. write R lands browser. CSS-agnostic — htmxr doesn’t depend CSS framework. Use Bootstrap, Tailwind, plain CSS, nothing . R-centric — logic lives R. htmx handles client-side interactions via HTML attributes — JavaScript required application code. htmltools re-exported — tags, div, p, etc. available directly htmxr, don’t need load htmltools separately.

title: Build Modern Web Applications with htmx and plumber2
text: Please note htmxr project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

title: Button element — hx_button
text: Creates <button> element optional htmx attributes.

title: Button element — hx_button
code: hx_button(   id,   label = NULL,   class = NULL,   get = NULL,   post = NULL,   put = NULL,   patch = NULL,   delete = NULL,   target = NULL,   swap = NULL,   trigger = NULL,   indicator = NULL,   swap_oob = NULL,   confirm = NULL,   params = NULL,   include = NULL,   push_url = NULL,   select = NULL,   vals = NULL,   encoding = NULL,   headers = NULL,   ... )

title: Button element — hx_button
text: id Element id. label Button label (text HTML content). Pass NULL icon-buttons — case supply aria-label via .... class Optional CSS class(es). get URL hx-get. post URL hx-post. put URL hx-put. patch URL hx-patch. delete URL hx-delete. Note: parameters sent URL query string (request body) — read via injected query argument (e.g. function(query) query$id) via request$query using full request object route. target CSS selector hx-target. swap Swap strategy hx-swap. trigger Trigger specification hx-trigger. indicator CSS selector hx-indicator. swap_oob --band swap targets hx-swap-oob. confirm Confirmation message hx-confirm. params Parameters submit hx-params. Use "*" include parameters (equivalent omitting argument), "none" send none, comma-separated list names (e.g. "id, name"). Prefix exclude specific parameters (e.g. "id, name"). include CSS selector hx-include. Additional elements whose values included request. htmx relative selectors valid: "closest form", "find input", "next .sibling". Note: params = "none" suppress values sourced via include. push_url Push URL browser history hx-push-url. Use "true" push request URL, "false" disable, custom URL. select CSS selector hx-select. Extracts specific element server response swapping (e.g. "#data-table"). vals JSON string extra values include request hx-vals (e.g. '{"id": 42}'). Values passed -. Avoid js: expressions HTML-special characters — htmltools escape . encoding Encoding type hx-encoding. Use "multipart/form-data" enable file uploads. headers JSON string request headers hx-headers (e.g. '{"X-Custom-Header": "value"}'). Values passed -. embed sensitive tokens HTML attributes. ... Additional HTML attributes passed <button> element.

title: Button element — hx_button
text: htmltools::tags object.

title: Button element — hx_button
code: # Simple button hx_button("btn1", "Click me") #> <button type="button" id="btn1">Click me</button>  # Button with htmx GET request hx_button("load-btn", "Load data", get = "/api/data", target = "#result") #> <button type="button" id="load-btn" hx-get="/api/data" hx-target="#result">Load data</button>  # Button with confirmation hx_button("del-btn", "Delete", post = "/api/delete", confirm = "Are you sure?") #> <button type="button" id="del-btn" hx-post="/api/delete" hx-confirm="Are you sure?">Delete</button>

what: Specify additional head elements for an htmxr page — hx_head
title: Specify additional head elements for an htmxr page — hx_head
text: Wraps tags included page head passed hx_page().

title: Specify additional head elements for an htmxr page — hx_head
code: hx_head(..., title = "htmxr page")

title: Specify additional head elements for an htmxr page — hx_head
text: ... tags include head (stylesheets, scripts, meta, etc.) title page title

title: Specify additional head elements for an htmxr page — hx_head
text: list class hx_head, passed hx_page().

title: Specify additional head elements for an htmxr page — hx_head
code: hx_head(title = "My app") #> [[1]] #> <title>My app</title> #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "hx_head"  hx_head(   title = "My app",   tags$link(rel = "stylesheet", href = "/style.css") ) #> [[1]] #> <link rel="stylesheet" href="/style.css"/> #>  #> [[2]] #> <title>My app</title> #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "hx_head"

what: Detect if a request comes from htmx — hx_is_htmx
title: Detect if a request comes from htmx — hx_is_htmx
text: Checks whether incoming HTTP request made htmx inspecting HX-Request header. htmx sends header every AJAX request.

title: Detect if a request comes from htmx — hx_is_htmx
code: hx_is_htmx(request)

title: Detect if a request comes from htmx — hx_is_htmx
text: request request object (e.g. plumber2 handler). Must headers element — named list character vector HTTP headers (lowercase keys, provided plumber2).

title: Detect if a request comes from htmx — hx_is_htmx
text: TRUE request made htmx, FALSE otherwise.

title: Detect if a request comes from htmx — hx_is_htmx
code: # Simulated htmx request req <- list(headers = list(`hx-request` = "true")) hx_is_htmx(req) #> [1] TRUE  # Regular request req <- list(headers = list()) hx_is_htmx(req) #> [1] FALSE

what: Generate a complete HTML page with htmx — hx_page
title: Generate a complete HTML page with htmx — hx_page
text: Generate complete HTML page htmx

title: Generate a complete HTML page with htmx — hx_page
code: hx_page(..., lang = "en", html_attrs = list())

title: Generate a complete HTML page with htmx — hx_page
text: ... page content. Use hx_head() add elements head. lang language code <html> element (default "en"). html_attrs named list additional attributes set <html> element (e.g. list("data-theme" = "cupcake") DaisyUI).

title: Generate a complete HTML page with htmx — hx_page
text: length-one character string containing full HTML document (including <!DOCTYPE html>), ready served HTTP response.

title: Generate a complete HTML page with htmx — hx_page
code:
hx_page(tags$h1("Hello, htmxr!")) #> [1] "

Hello, htmxr!

" hx_page( hx_head(title = "My app"), tags$p("Hello, world!") ) #> [1] " My app

Hello, world!

"

title: Run an htmxr example — hx_run_example
text: Launches example API demonstrates htmxr features. Call hx_run_example() without arguments list available examples.

title: Run an htmxr example — hx_run_example
code: hx_run_example(example = NULL, port = 8080)

title: Run an htmxr example — hx_run_example
text: example name example run. NULL, lists available examples. port port run API .

title: Run an htmxr example — hx_run_example
text: Called side effects. example NULL, returns available example names invisibly. Otherwise return (server blocks).

title: Run an htmxr example — hx_run_example
code: hx_run_example() # list available examples #> Available examples: delete-row, hello, infinity-scroll, json-endpoint, select-input, toast-notification #> Run one with: hx_run_example("hello") if (interactive()) {   hx_run_example("hello") # run the hello example }

title: Select input — hx_select_input
text: Creates <select> element optional htmx attributes. label provided, input wrapped <div> containing <label> element linked via attribute.

title: Select input — hx_select_input
code: hx_select_input(   id,   label = NULL,   choices,   selected = NULL,   multiple = FALSE,   name = id,   class = NULL,   get = NULL,   post = NULL,   put = NULL,   patch = NULL,   delete = NULL,   target = NULL,   swap = NULL,   trigger = NULL,   indicator = NULL,   swap_oob = NULL,   confirm = NULL,   params = NULL,   include = NULL,   push_url = NULL,   select = NULL,   vals = NULL,   encoding = NULL,   headers = NULL,   ... )

title: Select input — hx_select_input
text: id Element id. Also used name default. label Optional label text. provided, input wrapped <div> <label>. choices Named unnamed character vector choices. unnamed, values used labels. named, names used labels values option values (convention Shiny). selected Optional value(s) pre-select. multiple Logical. TRUE, adds multiple attribute allow multi-selection. name Form field name. Defaults id. class Optional CSS class(es) <select> element. get URL hx-get. post URL hx-post. put URL hx-put. patch URL hx-patch. delete URL hx-delete. Note: parameters sent URL query string (request body) — read via injected query argument (e.g. function(query) query$id) via request$query using full request object route. target CSS selector hx-target. swap Swap strategy hx-swap. trigger Trigger specification hx-trigger. indicator CSS selector hx-indicator. swap_oob --band swap targets hx-swap-oob. confirm Confirmation message hx-confirm. params Parameters submit hx-params. Use "*" include parameters (equivalent omitting argument), "none" send none, comma-separated list names (e.g. "id, name"). Prefix exclude specific parameters (e.g. "id, name"). include CSS selector hx-include. Additional elements whose values included request. htmx relative selectors valid: "closest form", "find input", "next .sibling". Note: params = "none" suppress values sourced via include. push_url Push URL browser history hx-push-url. Use "true" push request URL, "false" disable, custom URL. select CSS selector hx-select. Extracts specific element server response swapping (e.g. "#data-table"). vals JSON string extra values include request hx-vals (e.g. '{"id": 42}'). Values passed -. Avoid js: expressions HTML-special characters — htmltools escape . encoding Encoding type hx-encoding. Use "multipart/form-data" enable file uploads. headers JSON string request headers hx-headers (e.g. '{"X-Custom-Header": "value"}'). Values passed -. embed sensitive tokens HTML attributes. ... Additional HTML attributes passed <select> element.

title: Select input — hx_select_input
text: htmltools::tags object.

title: Select input — hx_select_input
code:
# Simple select without label hx_select_input("cut", choices = c("Fair", "Good", "Ideal")) #> # Select with label and named choices hx_select_input( "cut", label = "Filter by cut:", choices = c("All" = "all", "Fair", "Good", "Ideal"), selected = "all" ) #>
#> #> #>
# Select with htmx attributes hx_select_input( "cut", label = "Filter by cut:", choices = c("All" = "all", "Fair", "Good"), get = "/rows", trigger = "change", target = "#tbody" ) #>
#> #> #>

title: Serve htmxr static assets — hx_serve_assets
text: Configures plumber2 API serve htmxr's static assets (htmx JavaScript library) /htmxr/assets/.

title: Serve htmxr static assets — hx_serve_assets
code: hx_serve_assets(api)

title: Serve htmxr static assets — hx_serve_assets
text: api plumber2 API object

title: Serve htmxr static assets — hx_serve_assets
text: API object (modified, piping)

title: Serve htmxr static assets — hx_serve_assets
code: plumber2::api() |>   hx_serve_assets() #> ── A plumber server ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Serving on http://127.0.0.1:8080 #> Currently not running

what: Add htmx attributes to any HTML tag — hx_set
title: Add htmx attributes to any HTML tag — hx_set
text: generic modifier appends htmx attributes existing htmltools::tags object. Works HTML element.

title: Add htmx attributes to any HTML tag — hx_set
code: hx_set(   tag,   get = NULL,   post = NULL,   put = NULL,   patch = NULL,   delete = NULL,   target = NULL,   swap = NULL,   trigger = NULL,   indicator = NULL,   swap_oob = NULL,   confirm = NULL,   params = NULL,   include = NULL,   push_url = NULL,   select = NULL,   vals = NULL,   encoding = NULL,   headers = NULL,   ... )

title: Add htmx attributes to any HTML tag — hx_set
text: tag htmltools::tags object modify. get URL hx-get. post URL hx-post. put URL hx-put. patch URL hx-patch. delete URL hx-delete. Note: parameters sent URL query string (request body) — read via injected query argument (e.g. function(query) query$id) via request$query using full request object route. target CSS selector hx-target. swap Swap strategy hx-swap. trigger Trigger specification hx-trigger. indicator CSS selector hx-indicator. swap_oob --band swap targets hx-swap-oob. confirm Confirmation message hx-confirm. params Parameters submit hx-params. Use "*" include parameters (equivalent omitting argument), "none" send none, comma-separated list names (e.g. "id, name"). Prefix exclude specific parameters (e.g. "id, name"). include CSS selector hx-include. Additional elements whose values included request. htmx relative selectors valid: "closest form", "find input", "next .sibling", "previous .sibling". Note: params = "none" suppress values sourced via include — two operate independently. push_url Push URL browser history hx-push-url. Use "true" push request URL, "false" disable (e.g. override inheritance), custom URL string. select CSS selector hx-select. Extracts specific element server response swapping — useful server returns full HTML page fragment needed (e.g. "#data-table"). vals JSON string extra values include request hx-vals (e.g. '{"id": 42}'). Values passed -— serialisation performed. js: prefix dynamic expressions supported htmx expressions containing HTML-special characters (<, >, &, ") escaped htmltools silently corrupted runtime — avoid use DOM-based lookups instead. encoding Encoding type hx-encoding. Use "multipart/form-data" enable file uploads via <input type="file">. headers JSON string request headers hx-headers (e.g. '{"X-Custom-Header": "value"}'). Values passed -. embed sensitive tokens (auth headers, API keys) HTML attributes — readable script page. ... Additional htmx attributes passed -(e.g. `hx-disabled-elt` = "", `hx-prompt` = "Raison ?"). arguments must named. Names must start hx- data-hx- — warning emitted otherwise. Values passed without transformation: use "true"/"false" (TRUE/FALSE) boolean htmx attributes, pre-serialized JSON strings hx-vals hx-headers.

title: Add htmx attributes to any HTML tag — hx_set
text: input tag htmx attributes appended.

title: Add htmx attributes to any HTML tag — hx_set
code:
tags$div(id = "plot") |> hx_set(get = "/plot", trigger = "load", target = "#plot", swap = "innerHTML") #>
hx_set( tags$div(id = "result", class = "container"), get = "/data", trigger = "load" ) #>

title: Slider input — hx_slider_input
code:
# Simple slider hx_slider_input("bins", label = "Number of bins:", min = 1, max = 50) #>
#> #> #>
# Slider with htmx attributes hx_slider_input( "bins", label = "Number of bins:", value = 30, min = 1, max = 50, get = "/plot", trigger = "input changed delay:300ms", target = "#plot" ) #>
#> #> #>

title: Table with htmx-powered tbody — hx_table
text: Builds complete <table> element <thead> <tbody>. htmx attributes applied <tbody>, making swap target. data NULL (default), <tbody> empty content loaded lazily via htmx (e.g. trigger = "load").

title: Table with htmx-powered tbody — hx_table
code: hx_table(   columns,   data = NULL,   tbody_id = NULL,   col_labels = NULL,   col_classes = NULL,   class = NULL,   thead_class = NULL,   get = NULL,   post = NULL,   put = NULL,   patch = NULL,   delete = NULL,   target = NULL,   swap = NULL,   trigger = NULL,   indicator = NULL,   swap_oob = NULL,   confirm = NULL,   params = NULL,   include = NULL,   push_url = NULL,   select = NULL,   vals = NULL,   encoding = NULL,   headers = NULL,   ... )

title: Table with htmx-powered tbody — hx_table
text: columns Character vector column names display. Defines <thead> structure (required). data Optional data frame. provided, rows rendered <tbody> via hx_table_rows(). NULL, <tbody> empty. tbody_id id attribute applied <tbody> — <table>. col_labels Labels <thead>. NULL, column names used -. Can named vector (c(price = "Price ($)")) override specific columns, unnamed positional vector replace labels. col_classes Named list CSS classes <td> cells, passed hx_table_rows() data provided. class CSS class(es) <table> element. thead_class CSS class(es) <thead> element. get URL hx-get (applied <tbody>). post URL hx-post (applied <tbody>). put URL hx-put (applied <tbody>). patch URL hx-patch (applied <tbody>). delete URL hx-delete (applied <tbody>). Note: parameters sent URL query string — read via injected query argument (e.g. function(query) query$id) via request$query using full request object route. target CSS selector hx-target (applied <tbody>). swap Swap strategy hx-swap (applied <tbody>). trigger Trigger specification hx-trigger (applied <tbody>). indicator CSS selector hx-indicator (applied <tbody>). swap_oob --band swap targets hx-swap-oob (applied <tbody>). confirm Confirmation message hx-confirm (applied <tbody>). params Parameters submit hx-params. Use "*" include parameters (equivalent omitting argument), "none" send none, comma-separated list names (e.g. "id, name"). Prefix exclude specific parameters (e.g. "id, name"). include CSS selector hx-include. Additional elements whose values included request. htmx relative selectors valid: "closest form", "find input", "next .sibling". Note: params = "none" suppress values sourced via include. push_url Push URL browser history hx-push-url (applied <tbody>). Use "true", "false", custom URL. select CSS selector hx-select (applied <tbody>). Extracts specific element server response swapping. vals JSON string extra values hx-vals (applied <tbody>). Values passed -(e.g. '{"id": 42}'). Avoid js: expressions HTML-special characters — htmltools escape . encoding Encoding type hx-encoding (applied <tbody>). Use "multipart/form-data" enable file uploads. headers JSON string request headers hx-headers (applied <tbody>). Values passed -. embed sensitive tokens HTML attributes. ... Additional HTML attributes passed <table> element.

title: Table with htmx-powered tbody — hx_table
text: htmltools::tags object (<table>).

title: Table with htmx-powered tbody — hx_table
code:
# Lazy-load table (empty tbody, content loaded on trigger) hx_table( columns = c("cut", "color", "price"), col_labels = c("Cut", "Color", "Price"), tbody_id = "tbody", get = "/rows", trigger = "load", swap = "innerHTML" ) #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #>
CutColorPrice
# Table with data pre-rendered df <- data.frame(cut = c("Fair", "Good"), price = c(326L, 400L)) hx_table(columns = c("cut", "price"), data = df) #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #>
cutprice
Fair326
Good400

title: Table rows fragment — hx_table_rows
text: Converts data frame tagList <tr> elements, one per row. Designed used fragment endpoint response — output replaces <tbody> via htmx swap.

title: Table rows fragment — hx_table_rows
code: hx_table_rows(data, columns = NULL, col_classes = NULL)

title: Table rows fragment — hx_table_rows
text: data data frame. columns Character vector column names include (order). NULL, columns used. col_classes Named list CSS classes add <td> elements, keyed column name. Example: list(price = "text-end fw-bold").

title: Table rows fragment — hx_table_rows
text: htmltools::tagList <tr> tags.

title: Table rows fragment — hx_table_rows
code: df <- data.frame(cut = c("Fair", "Good"), price = c(326L, 400L)) hx_table_rows(df, columns = c("cut", "price")) #> #> Fair #> 326 #> #> #> Good #> 400 #> # With CSS classes on specific columns hx_table_rows(df, col_classes = list(price = "text-end fw-bold")) #> #> Fair #> 326 #> #> #> Good #> 400 #>

what: Trigger client-side htmx events via response headers — hx_trigger
title: Trigger client-side htmx events via response headers — hx_trigger
text: Adds HX-Trigger, HX-Trigger--Swap, HX-Trigger--Settle header plumber2 response, causing htmx fire one client-side events response received.

title: Trigger client-side htmx events via response headers — hx_trigger
code: hx_trigger(response, event)  hx_trigger_after_swap(response, event)  hx_trigger_after_settle(response, event)

title: Trigger client-side htmx events via response headers — hx_trigger
text: response plumber2 response object. parameter must named response route handlers — name plumber2 recognises injecting response object. event One : character string — fires single event: "myEvent". character vector — fires multiple events: c("event1", "event2"). named list — fires events detail payloads: list(showMessage = list(level = "info"), confetti = NULL). name event; value detail (use NULL detail).

title: Trigger client-side htmx events via response headers — hx_trigger
text: response object response, invisibly.

title: Trigger client-side htmx events via response headers — hx_trigger
text: hx_trigger() — fires immediately response received (HX-Trigger). hx_trigger_after_swap() — fires htmx swaps new content DOM (HX-Trigger--Swap). Use event handler needs interact freshly-swapped elements. hx_trigger_after_settle() — fires htmx settles (CSS transitions complete) (HX-Trigger--Settle).

title: Trigger client-side htmx events via response headers — hx_trigger
text: event named list, values serialised JSON using minimal built-serialiser supports NULL, logicals, numbers, strings, named lists . external dependency required.

title: Trigger client-side htmx events via response headers — hx_trigger
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ #* @post /submit function(response) {   # Simple event   hx_trigger(response, "formSubmitted")    # Multiple events   hx_trigger(response, c("formSubmitted", "refresh"))    # Event with detail payload   hx_trigger(response, list(showMessage = list(level = "info", text = "Saved!")))    list(status = "ok") } } # }

what: Objects exported from other packages — reexports
title: Objects exported from other packages — reexports
text: objects imported packages. Follow links see documentation. htmltools , br, code, div, em, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, hr, img, p, pre, span, strong, tag, tagList, tags

title: htmxr 0.3.0
text: hx_set() now accepts CRUD verbs named parameters: put, patch, delete (addition existing get post). New named parameters across hx_set() components (hx_button(), hx_select_input(), hx_slider_input(), hx_table()): params, include, push_url, select, vals, encoding, headers — covering commonly used htmx core attributes named parameters. hx_set() now accepts ... escape hatch raw htmx attributes covered named parameters (e.g. `hx-disabled-elt` = "", `hx-prompt` = "Reason?"). Names must start hx- data-hx-. New example delete-row — demonstrates vals attribute: delete button embeds row id JSON via hx-vals, form hidden input needed.

title: htmxr 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-06

title: htmxr 0.2.0
text: htmxr_is_htmx() renamed hx_is_htmx() follow hx_ prefix convention applied exported functions. hx_button() parameter order changed: id now first required (label). Update calls like hx_button("Click ") hx_button("-btn", label = "Click "). hx_table() parameter id renamed tbody_id clarify id applied <tbody>, <table>.

title: htmxr 0.2.0
text: hx_trigger() — adds HX-Trigger response header plumber2 response, causing htmx fire client-side event immediately response. hx_trigger_after_swap() — , fires htmx swaps new content DOM (HX-Trigger--Swap). hx_trigger_after_settle() — , fires htmx settles (HX-Trigger--Settle). event argument accepts character vector (multiple events) named list (events JSON detail payloads), serialised without external dependencies.

title: htmxr 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-04 Fix invalid ‘plumber2’ URL vignette (pointing non-existent GitHub repository, now points https://plumber2.posit.co/). Quote technical terms DESCRIPTION comply CRAN spell check conventions. Add Depends: R (>= 4.1.0) native pipe |> compatibility.

title: htmxr 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN release.
